records.name: 1.1 Mississippi / North Delta Labels

location: Oxford / Water Valley, MS (founded Oxford; offices now Oxford)
Oxford, MS (imprint of Fat Possum Records)
founded: 2009 (as a Fat Possum studio by Bruce Watson); bought by Matt Patton (Drive-By Truckers) in 2016
1967 (as Malaco Attractions); recording label operations from mid-1970s
genre_focus: Indie rock, North Mississippi hill country blues, hip-hop, alternative
North Mississippi blues, hill country blues, swamp blues, roots
Roots rock, blues-adjacent, Southern alternative, country punk
Southern soul, soul-blues, gospel, R&B — self-described "The Last Soul Company"
Southern soul-blues, soul, gospel — primarily the "chitlin' circuit" / Southern soul market
key_personnel: Matthew Johnson (oversees as Fat Possum president)
Tommy Couch Sr. (founder); current management includes Malaco Music Group leadership (not publicly named in 2024–25 sources)
fields.roster_highlights_(current): Ghais Guevara, Will Epstein, Haley Heynderickx & Max Garcia Conover, Jake Xerxes Fussell & James Elkington, Hand Habits, Youth Lagoon, The Weather Station; historic catalog includes R.L. Burnside, Junior Kimbrough, T-Model Ford, Fred McDowell, The Black Keys
submission_policy: *Accepts unsolicited material* — mail demo (up to 4 songs) to: Fat Possum Records, P.O. Box 1923, Oxford, MS 38655 · Phone: 662-234-2828 · Email: `matthew@fatpossum.com`
Through Fat Possum (above). Big Legal Mess focuses on catalog/reissues and occasional new signings in the blues-roots vein.
Not published — contact directly via email
fields.recent_release_(2025): Jake Xerxes Fussell & James Elkington, *Rebuilding* (2025); Ghais Guevara, *Goyard Ibn Said* (2025)
fields.distribution: Fat Possum self-distributes; back catalog through various
note: Label has migrated away from pure blues toward indie; Big Legal Mess is the blues/roots imprint

Bobby Rush departed Malaco/Waldoxy and now releases on his own Deep Rush Records / Thirty Tigers. *Young Fashioned Ways* (March 2025, RAM Records/Deep Rush/Thirty Tigers) — 2024 Grammy winner, Best Traditional Blues Album
roster_highlights: R.L. Burnside, Junior Kimbrough, T-Model Ford, Jimbo Mathus, Jack Oblivian, Jimmy "Duck" Holmes, Leo Welch, Kenny Brown, Reverend John Wilkins, Alvin Youngblood Hart, Robert Finley, Bette Smith, Bobby Bare Jr., John Paul Keith, Water Liars, Fred McDowell (archival), Houston Stackhouse (archival), Furry Lewis (archival), George Mitchell Collection

O.B. Buchana, Sheba Potts-Wright, Ms. Jody, Carl Sims, David Brinston, Val McKnight, Rick Lawson, Barbara Carr, Quinn Golden, Pokey Bear (distribution), Denise LaSalle (catalog), Rufus Thomas (catalog); Gospel wing: The Gospel Miracles, Minister Charles Miller
fields.recent_release_(2024\u201325): Ongoing reissue series; Reverend John Wilkins active on roster
fields.notable_producers_who_have_worked_here: Bruce Watson, Simone Felice, Barrett Martin, Patterson Hood, Lydia Loveless, Eric Ambel
fields.label_roster_(current): Bark (*Loud*, 2025), Eleganza (*Water Valley High*, 2025), Hans Condor (*Breaking & Entering*, 2025), Laura Jane Grace & The Mississippi Medicals (*Give An Inch* EP, 2025), Model Citizen (*Live at Dial Back Sound*, 2025), Pope Paul & The Illegals, Taylor Hollingsworth (*Yahola*, 2025), Dash Rip Rock (*A Song In Everyone*, Jan 2026), Drunken Prayer, Krista Shows (forthcoming 2026), Williamson Brothers (forthcoming)
fields.notable_studio_clients: Drive-By Truckers, MJ Lenderman, The Seratones, Dom Flemons, Jerry Joseph, Jack Oblivian, Jimbo Mathus
fields.2026_vinyl_package: Annual subscription LP series (3–5 records/year, 140g vinyl)
fields.status: No active independent label confirmed under this name in Oxford, MS as of 2024–2026. "Bible Belt Sinners" (a band, not a label) exists but is inactive as a recording act. Searches returned no operational label under this name in the corridor.
fields.note_for_users: If sourcing for a specific tip about "Bible Belt Records Oxford," verify directly with local Oxford music community contacts (e.g., Proud Larry's, The Lyric Oxford). The name may refer to a micro-label or informal imprint not publicly listed.
fields.roster_highlights_(historic_and_catalog): Z.Z. Hill, Bobby "Blue" Bland, Little Milton, Tyrone Davis, Denise LaSalle, Johnnie Taylor, Dorothy Moore, Mississippi Mass Choir; multiple gospel sub-labels (Malaco Gospel, Jackson Sound, Savoy Gospel); Waldoxy imprint handled Bobby Rush (now on Deep Rush/Thirty Tigers)
fields.sub-labels: Waldoxy Records (soul-blues), Malaco Gospel, Jackson Sound, Malaco Jazz, Freedom Records (Christian), Malaco Christian Distribution
fields.studios: Malaco Studios, Jackson, MS — professional facility available for outside bookings
fields.submission_contact: Contact form at malaco.com; no public A&R email posted
fields.new_releases_(2024\u201325): Ongoing singles/albums across soul-blues catalog; *Club Booty* (David Brinston), *Let's Get This Party Started* (Donnie Ray), *I Love My Blues* (Gerod Rayborn) among recent
fields.contact: No public submission policy listed; John Ward reachable through label website or Memphis music community

notes: Matthew Johnson — President (`matthew@fatpossum.com`)
Bruce Watson — Director, A&R (now primarily with Dial Back Sound; formerly Fat Possum)

fields.demo_submission_policy_(mail_only): Send up to 4 songs (physical media, no email), include SASE for reply. Address: Alligator Records, New Material, P.O. Box 60234, Chicago, IL 60660. Response time: 3+ months. No phone inquiries.

records.name: 1.5 Historically Notable / Status Notes

status: Effectively closed October 2021 after internal turmoil (sexual harassment allegations, financial disputes). *Variety* reported the shutdown Oct 19, 2021. Scott H. Biram (Texas-based but frequent corridor performer) noted the label was "back up" in Fall 2022 with new website and contacts, but activity remains minimal.
fields.verdict: Treat as inactive for new business purposes as of 2024–26. Former Southern artists include Scott H. Biram, Cory Branan (Nashville), Robbie Fulks.
fields.yazoo: Blues reissue label, founded 1969 by Nick Perls; acquired by Shanachie Entertainment. Specializes in pre-WWII country blues, Delta, Piedmont. No new signings — purely archival. Contact: Shanachie Entertainment, Newton, NJ.
fields.revenant_records: Collector/archival label; known for the 7-CD *Screamin' and Hollerin' the Blues* (Charley Patton box set, Grammy winner 2001). Run by John Fahey associate Dean Blackwood and Pavement Music. Essentially inactive for new releases.
fields.use_case: Licensing/sync of classic Delta masters; not relevant for artist development.
fields.location: Severn, MD (not corridor-based, but books/records Southern artists)
fields.southern_artist_connection: Bryan Lee (New Orleans-based guitarist, *Play One for Me*, Severn 2013); Sugar Ray & The Bluetones; The Fabulous Thunderbirds; Mud Morganfield (son of Muddy Waters, Clarksdale-area connection). Note: Bryan Lee passed away 2019; Sugar Ray moved to Natural Records.
fields.note: Limited direct Mississippi corridor relevance for new signings, though serves as placement option for blues artists touring the East Coast/South.
fields.note_on_greg_landau: Greg Landau is a San Francisco-based producer (Latin American focus; Grammy-nominated; UC San Diego doctorate). His work is primarily in Latin jazz, Afro-Cuban, and Latin American styles. He does not operate a Delta-focused label or foundation in the corridor. The "Little Village Foundation" referenced in the task prompt likely refers to a different entity or a confusion with another organization.
fields.little_village_foundation_(fresno,_ca): A blues/roots nonprofit that organizes artists in the Central Valley of California — not a Mississippi corridor entity.
fields.recommendation: This entry should be removed or clarified in the directory; no active "Little Village Foundation" with Delta/Southern focus confirmed.

notes: *Sources: [Variety, Oct 2021](https://variety.com/2021/music/news/bloodshot-records-shuts-down-indie-label-1235092594/); [Scott H. Biram site](https://www.scotthbiram.com/news/the-lowdown-on-what-happened-with-shb-amp-bloodshot-records); [bloodshotrecords.com](https://bloodshotrecords.com)*
*Sources: [Severn Records Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Severn_Records); [severnrecords.com](https://severnrecords.com)*
*Sources: [Greg Landau website](https://greglandau.com/mystory); [Wikipedia Greg Landau](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greg_Landau)*

records.name: 2.1 Nashville-Based Publishers with Southern/Blues Relevance

location: 705 President Ronald Reagan Way (physical), P.O. Box 120904, Nashville, TN 37212
focus: Country, roots, pop — administration and licensing; not exclusively blues but handles diverse American song catalog
Country, Americana, singer-songwriter; publishing arm tied to label
fields.notable_catalog: David Lynn Jones ("Living in the Promiseland," cut by Willie Nelson — label's first #1), Trisha Yearwood, Mark Chesnutt, George Strait, Patty Loveless, The Mavericks, Diamond Rio, LeAnn Rimes
fields.services: Publishing administration, licensing (sync, print, digital), royalty collection; represents thousands of songs across genres
fields.blues_southern_sync_connection: Richard Busch (King & Ballow) has represented Bluewater in litigation against Spotify — indicating active catalog management and enforcement posture
note: Primarily a Nashville country/pop administrator; engage for sync licensing on classic American song catalog, not as a blues-specific publisher
Primarily mainstream country Nashville — limited direct blues/Southern roots focus. Relevant if pitching country-adjacent Americana writers
Not a Southern/blues-focused publisher; corridor relevance limited
fields.published_songwriters_artists_(current): Mitchell Tenpenny (*Drunk Me*, #1 country hit), Dillon Carmichael, Meghan Patrick, Stephen Day, The Wldlfe, Ash Ruder
fields.status_as_of_january_2026: Acquired by Sony Music Publishing Nashville (effective Jan 13, 2026). No longer independent.
fields.historical_background: Independent Nashville publisher/artist developer; accumulated 41 #1 songs, Song of the Year awards (ACM, BMI, ASCAP); focused on Nashville country/pop
fields.post-acquisition: Catalog and writer roster now managed under Sony Music Publishing Nashville

notes: *Sources: [Bluewater Music website](https://bluewatermusic.com); [Music Publishers Directory PDF](https://www.weekly-bulletin.com/pdf/industrycontacts/Nov23_Publishing_Guide.pdf); [King & Ballow Richard Busch page](https://kingballow.com/attorneys/richard-busch/)*

records.name: 2.2 Major Catalog Publishers (Blues/Southern Sync)

fields.catalog: Over 1 million copyrighted works, 300,000+ active sound recordings. Blues-relevant catalog includes: **Stax Records** (Otis Redding, Sam & Dave, Booker T. & the MGs, Albert King — major Memphis soul/blues); **Bluesville Records** (relaunched 2024, John Lee Hooker, Skip James, country blues archival); **Rounder Records** (Americana, bluegrass, some NOLA); **Fantasy, Hightone, Ric/Ron, Riverside, Savoy, Specialty, Telarc, Testament, Trix, Vanguard, Vee Jay**
fields.bluesville_relaunch_(2024): Craft Recordings revived Bluesville label June 2024 with John Lee Hooker reissue (*Burning Hell*, 1964) and Skip James (*Today!*, 1966 Vanguard, July 2024); plus streaming playlists and digitization of archival blues holdings
sync_use: Major option for sync licensing of blues classics (Albert King, Stax catalog). For new artist placement at Concord labels, approach through A&R at individual label imprints.
For licensing of Willie Dixon compositions (core Chicago/Delta blues songwriting), Muddy Waters, SRV
fields.relevant_catalog: BMG administers estates of Willie Dixon, Muddy Waters, Stevie Ray Vaughan, Townes Van Zandt, Gram Parsons, Woody Guthrie, Johnny Cash, T-Bone Burnett, Nick Cave, Iggy Pop, Los Lobos — major blues and Southern publishing holdings
fields.contact: info.us@bmg.com · One Park Avenue, New York, NY 10016 · (212) 561-3000

notes: *Sources: [Concord licensing page](https://concord.com/licensing/); [Craft/Bluesville relaunch, Rock and Roll Globe, June 2024](https://rockandrollglobe.com/blues/bluesville-records-has-been-relaunched/); [Concord About page](https://concord.com/about/)*
*Source: [Music Publishers Directory PDF](https://www.weekly-bulletin.com/pdf/industrycontacts/Nov23_Publishing_Guide.pdf)*

records.name: 2.3 Sync Houses for Southern Documentary Film

Boutique, personally attentive; relevant for Southern roots mid-career artists seeking full-service management + booking
fields.action: Remove from active directory; note historical significance as major Nashville country management firm.
fields.status_as_of_february_2026: Madison House founder/key agents partnered with Dynamic Talent International in a strategic merger (announced Feb 24–25, 2026), bringing 110+ clients to Dynamic's roster of 600.

fields.clients_include: Webb Wilder, Geoff Achison, Yonrico Scott, Randall Bramblett, Peter Karp, Glenn Phillips/Cindy Wilson (B-52s)

location: Charlotte, NC area (founded; now may operate remotely — confirm current address)
fields.current_roster_(2024\u20132026): Altered Five Blues Band, Andy Thomas, Blues Royalty (John Primer & Kenny Neal), Bywater Call (Canadian, but frequent Southern touring), Carolyn Wonderland, Chambers/DesLauriers (Annika Chambers & Paul DesLauriers), Chris Cain, The Cold Stares, Curtis Salgado, Derrick Dove & The Peacekeepers, Indigenous, Jimmy Carpenter, Joanna Connor, John Mooney (New Orleans), John Németh, Jovin Webb, Kenny Neal (Baton Rouge, LA), Lil' Ed & The Blues Imperials, Mother's Finest, Mr. Sipp (Castro Coleman, Mississippi), Nikki Hill, Parker Barrow, Popa Chubby, Quinn Sullivan, Sam Morrow, Selwyn Birchwood, Simplified, The Steepwater Band, Sugaray Rayford, Toronzo Cannon, Zach Person

fields.note: The dominant U.S. blues booking agency; 2025 Blues Music Award nominees across multiple categories
fields.focus: Blues and roots artists exclusively; 19+ year track record as of KBA recognition
fields.historical_background: Steve Hecht founded Piedmont Talent in 1990 in Charlotte; Rick Booth worked for Hecht before launching Intrepid Artists in 1994. Hecht received the Blues Foundation's "Keeping the Blues Alive" award.
fields.current_status_(2024\u201326): Not heavily documented in current searches — may be operating at reduced scale or retired. Verify by contacting Blues Foundation or via Charlotte blues community contacts.

records.name: 5.1 Nashville

address: 26 Century Blvd., Ste. NT 700, Nashville, TN 37214 (also 315 Union St., Suite 1100, Nashville, TN 37201)
1600 West End Ave., Suite 1400, Nashville, TN 37203
26 Century Blvd., Ste. NT 700, Nashville, TN 37214 / 315 Union St., Suite 1100, Nashville, TN 37201
role: Partner; Head of Entertainment and Intellectual Property Sections
Partner in Charge, Nashville Music Row office
specialties: Copyright infringement litigation, music publishing, royalty disputes, digital licensing
Music industry transactional, entertainment contracts, copyright, intellectual property; artists, publishers, songwriters, producers, record labels, non-profits with music element
Entertainment law (music publishing, recording, artist contracts, copyright), labor & employment, litigation; offices Nashville and Los Angeles
profile: Named Billboard's Top Music Lawyers (2020); led landmark *FBT Productions v. Aftermath Records* (2010, digital royalty ruling); represented Megan Thee Stallion; represented Bluewater Music and Songwriter's Guild of America in Spotify mechanical licensing litigation; represents publishers against major streaming services. Licensed in TN, NY, CA; admitted to U.S. Supreme Court and multiple circuit courts.

Chambers USA Band 2 (Media & Entertainment, Tennessee); Best Lawyers® Lawyer of the Year in Entertainment Law – Nashville (2013, 2018); recognized Best Lawyers® in Entertainment Law – Music, Copyright, IP since 2008; Super Lawyers 2007–2024. Former litigator, Southern California; moved to Nashville 1992. Represented Nashville Symphony as entertainment counsel 10+ years; legal counsel to Gospel Music Association and Christian Music Trade Association.
note: Primarily a litigation specialist, not a transactional/deal lawyer. Best suited for copyright disputes, not artist contract negotiation.
Highly recommended for independent labels, publishers, and mid-career artists in the Nashville orbit; handles both transactional and IP.
One of the most prominent Nashville entertainment law firms; Meritas Law Firms Worldwide member
fields.founded: 1969 by Frank S. King Jr. and Robert L. Ballow

notes: *Sources: [King & Ballow Richard Busch page](https://kingballow.com/attorneys/richard-busch/); [Rolling Stone profile, Jul 2020](https://www.rollingstone.com/pro/features/richard-busch-music-blurred-lines-lawyer-1031126/); [Lawdragon](https://www.lawdragon.com/lawyer-limelights/2012-01-05-richard-busch)*
*Sources: [Adams & Reese Lynn Morrow profile](https://www.adamsandreese.com/people/lynn-morrow); [Super Lawyers profile](https://profiles.superlawyers.com/tennessee/nashville/lawyer/lynn-morrow/)*
*Sources: [King & Ballow About](https://kingballow.com/about-us/); [King & Ballow Entertainment Law](https://kingballow.com/practice-areas/entertainment-law/)*

records.name: 5.2 Memphis / Tennessee–Mississippi

fields.specialties: Entertainment contracts, copyright, trademark; advises in both Mississippi and Tennessee. Has represented "well-known entertainment figures in entertainment related matters involving television personalities, rock and roll performers, country music personalities, professional songwriters, event promoters, television and record producer consultation, representation in personal service contract negotiations"
fields.courts: Tennessee (statewide), Mississippi (statewide), including Shelby County (Memphis), DeSoto County (Hernando), Marshall County (Holly Springs)
fields.note: One of very few attorneys documented as handling entertainment law across both the Tennessee and Mississippi sides of the corridor; accessible to independent artists; full-service regional firm

notes: *Sources: [Hodum Law entertainment page](https://www.hodumlaw.com/entertainment-law/); [Charles Hodum attorney bio](https://www.hodumlaw.com/charles-e-hodum-attorney/)*

specialties: Entertainment law — recording contracts, licensing agreements, copyright, intellectual property, film/TV sync, music publisher and label representation, digital services agreements; also civil litigation and business law
Entertainment law — copyright, IP, contract negotiation, licensing, personal injury referrals; serves musicians, producers, voice-over actors, thespians in Louisiana
fields.profile: Worked as counsel for both owners of the New Orleans Saints (Tom Benson); hosts "All Things Legal" on WWL 870AM/105.3FM. Justia-listed as top NOLA entertainment attorney.
note: The most directly named and accessible entertainment attorney in the NOLA area; handles musicians, record labels, producers, TV/radio channels

Boutique NOLA entertainment firm; handles individual musicians and small production entities

fields.organization: Arts & Business Council of Greater Nashville
fields.services: Pro bono legal services for income-qualified artists and arts nonprofits in Tennessee — contract drafting/review, copyright, trademark, nonprofit formation, arts-related dispute resolution
fields.eligibility: Must be Tennessee resident, arts-related issue, income-qualified (see intake form); nonprofit arts orgs under $1M annual budget
fields.contact: Complete intake form on VLPA website
fields.note: Critical resource for emerging artists without resources for full legal representation
